Kyogre

#0381 Latios #0383 Groudon
  • Contents
  • Info
  • Base stats
  • Evolution chart
  • Pokédex entries
  • Moves learned
  • Sprites
  • Locations
  • Language

Kyogre is a Water type Pokémon introduced in Generation 3.

Kyogre Primal Kyogre

Additional artwork

Pokédex data

National № 0382
Type Water
Species Sea Basin Pokémon
Height 4.5 m (14′09″)
Weight 352.0 kg (776.0 lbs)
Abilities 1. Drizzle
Local № 0198 (Ruby/Sapphire/Emerald)
0207 (Omega Ruby/Alpha Sapphire)
0128 (Mega Dimension)

Training

EV yield 3 Sp. Atk
Catch rate 3 (0.4% with PokéBall, full HP)
Base Friendship 0 (lower than normal)
Base Exp. 335
Growth Rate Slow

Breeding

Egg Groups Undiscovered
Gender Genderless
Egg cycles 120 (30,584–30,840 steps)

Evolution chart

  • Kyogre does not evolve.

Kyogre changes

  • From Pokémon Ruby & Sapphire to Pokémon X & Y, Kyogre has a catch rate of 5.
  • In Generations 3-4, Kyogre has a base experience yield of 218.
  • In Generations 5-7, Kyogre has a base experience yield of 302.

Pokédex entries

Ruby KYOGRE has the power to create massive rain clouds that cover the entire sky and bring about torrential downpours. This POKéMON saved people who were suffering from droughts.
Sapphire KYOGRE is named in mythology as the POKéMON that expanded the sea by covering the land with torrential rains and towering tidal waves. It took to sleep after a cataclysmic battle with GROUDON.
FireRed
LeafGreen		 This POKéMON is said to have expanded the sea by bringing heavy rains. It has the power to control water.
Emerald KYOGRE has appeared in mythology as the creator of the sea. After long years of feuding with GROUDON, it took to sleep at the bottom of the sea.
Diamond
Pearl
Platinum		 It is said to have widened the seas by causing downpours. It had been asleep in a marine trench.
HeartGold
SoulSilver		 A mythical Pokémon said to have swelled the seas with rain and tidal waves. It battled with GROUDON.
Black
White
Black 2
White 2		 It is said to have widened the seas by causing downpours. It had been asleep in a marine trench.
X A mythical Pokémon said to have swelled the seas with rain and tidal waves. It battled with Groudon.
Y It is said to have widened the seas by causing downpours. It had been asleep in a marine trench.
Omega Ruby Through Primal Reversion and with nature’s full power, it will take back its true form. It can summon storms that cause the sea levels to rise.
Alpha Sapphire Kyogre is said to be the personification of the sea itself. Legends tell of its many clashes against Groudon, as each sought to gain the power of nature.
Brilliant Diamond
Shining Pearl		 It is said to have widened the seas by causing downpours. It had been asleep in a marine trench.

Answers to Kyogre questions

  • What is a good moveset for Kyogre?
  • If groudon and kyogre fought what would the weather be like?
  • OHKOs did not affect Rayquaza - is this normal?
  • Can you change Groudon/Kyorge into their Primal Form and Mega Evolve a Pokemon in the same battle?
  • Does Kyogre speak with a regional accent in other translations of Pokopia?
  • What would happen if Primal Groudon was up against Primal Kyogre?
  • Are Groudon and Kyogre Shiny Locked in Omega Ruby and Alpha Sapphire?
  • Why does Origin Pulse have a lower base power than Precipice Blades?
  • Does using Fire-type moves that "melt the ice" still thaw you out if used while Primal Kyogre is on the field?

  • View more questions on PokéBase »

Other languages

English Kyogre
Japanese カイオーガ (Kyogre)
German Kyogre
French Kyogre
Italian Kyogre
Spanish Kyogre
Korean 가이오가 (gaioga)
Chinese (Simplified) 盖欧卡
Chinese (Traditional) 蓋歐卡

 

English Sea Basin Pokémon
Japanese かいていポケモン
German Seegründler-Pokémon
French Pokémon Bassinmarin
Italian Pokémon Oceano
Spanish Pokémon Cuenca Mar

Chinese (Simplified) 海底宝可梦
Chinese (Traditional) 海底寶可夢

Name origin

kai-oh
Japanese for ‘sea king’
ogre
large monster from folklore
#0381 Latios #0383 Groudon
